claims.claimText: Middle Phalanx Fracture evaluation should anchor on mechanism, focused exam, and the anatomic structures most at risk (middle-phalanx, pip-joint, dip-joint).
Missing middle-phalanx assessment in middle phalanx fracture is a common board and OITE trap.
Shared hand-wrist anatomy (middle-phalanx, pip-joint, dip-joint) links Middle Phalanx Fracture to adjacent Prepare neighborhoods in the cluster graph.
Treating middle phalanx fracture in isolation without checking related wrist, tendon, or nerve neighborhoods in the cluster.
Document neurovascular status, key anatomic structures (middle-phalanx, pip-joint, dip-joint), stability assessment, and escalation plan for middle phalanx fracture.
Obtain appropriate hand/wrist imaging for middle phalanx fracture and explicitly comment on articular alignment and adjacent joint stability.
Early protected motion and therapy goals for middle phalanx fracture should align with tendon, ligament, and joint stability constraints.

decisionPoints.trigger: Unstable pattern, displacement, failed closed treatment, or functional deficit in middle phalanx fracture
decisionPoints.action: Proceed with operative pathway when indicated; document fixation strategy, tendon/nerve protection, and postoperative plan

decisionPoints.trigger: Stable pattern with acceptable alignment and intact neurovascular exam for middle phalanx fracture
decisionPoints.action: Splint/immobilize with therapy plan, repeat exam and imaging, and clear return precautions
